translated: The women went and told the disciples that Jesus was risen, and the disciples did not believe the women, but Peter ran and entered the tomb and saw it.

translated: The woman and her child went to the farm, and when they got there, they saw a certain tree with a beehive, so the woman told her child to go and cut the tree, but the child refused and the woman went and cut it herself!

ref: Simon and the others started looking for him.

ref: What did you really go out to see? Was he a prophet? He certainly was. I tell you that he was more than a prophet.
text: Mibua miɣere me nnɛ misɛ mianyɔ. Ɣaa kanyaɖidze? Gbaã nto loɣere mi sɔ ɔɖo Ɣaa kanyaɖidze.

text: Yesu ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Ɣaa ɔbara Iwarãyi ɔtã ɔturi isɛɛ, ɔ̃ibara ɔturi ɔtã Iwarãyi.
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Where is the home of Iwarãyi, where is his village, his home is in Iwarãyi.

text: Kakpɛkɔ̃ nɖe kayiiso. Mma nɖe Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃ maturi nɖe ɔraɖui gɔ mapɛ i kakpɛkɔ̃ ame. Tsirise masiaidze nɖe sikua.

ref: Not everyone who calls me their Lord will get into the kingdom of heaven. Only the ones who obey my Father in heaven will get in.
text: “Iiɖe ɔbiara gɔ nsɛ ɔɣere me sɔ, ‘Bosate, Bosate,’ loakɛlɛ Ɣaa sigarakaɖekɔ̃. Ɣɛɛ mma nto mabara wũ Ɔse gɔ i kato kuɖɔɛ loasɛ mmɔ.

ref: Your heart will always be where your treasure is. Light (Luke
text: Alasɔ ngbegɔ i fɔ karana se ne, mmɔ i fɔ ɔtu ana aawe ne. Yesu Ɔte Sɔ Anɔmi Nɖe Kuturidziri Ikpawaĩ.
translated: When the time comes for me to leave this world, you will weep and mourn, but the world will rejoice. Jesus Christ the Savior of Humanity

ref: You Pharisees and teachers are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You're like tombs that have been whitewashed. On the outside they are beautiful, but inside they are full of bones and filth.
text: “Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Mi ma nsɛ mibara ara sɔ si mibo maturi anɔ! Mise lɛ maturi akpibiɔ wa matsue makua kukaakɔ, ɣɛɛ wã ame ɔyi ku makpise buria ku akui.
translated: You Pharisees clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside you are full of greed and wickedness! You foolish people! You clean the outside of the cup, but inside you are dirty, full of robbery and wickedness.

ref: But Jesus came over and touched them. He said, “Get up and don't be afraid!”
text: Yesu ɔba ma kɔrɛ, ɔpɛgu ma ne ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Mita miya ne midaanigã.”
translated: Jesus said to them, "My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to complete his work."

ref: Large crowds came and brought many people who were paralyzed or blind or lame or unable to talk. They placed them, and many others, in front of Jesus, and he healed them all.
text: Maturi gbodzoo ɔba ɔ̃ kɔrɛ. Gɔ mato maba ne, masɛgu mma lofɛfɛ̃ mafu, manɔbiɛdze, mabafã, mamumu gu manyɛse agbãagbã. Makɔ ma mabɔ Yesu katɔ̃.
translated: The elders went to the farm. They saw a woman there, a certain woman who was very beautiful, had many children, was a good wife, and was a good farmer, and had many good qualities. They went to tell Yesu about it.

ref: Jesus replied, “You certainly will drink from my cup! But it isn't for me to say who will sit at my right side and at my left. This is for my Father to say.”
text: Mmɔ i Yesu to ɔɣere ma sɔ, “Gbaã iɖe sɔ mito miawo. Ɣɛɛ ne, lona ɔri sɔ loaɖi mma loasɛ i wũ kuɖearɔ̃ gu kubena iso. Kusɛkɔ̃ gɔmɔ ne, mma i wũ Ɔse ɔledza ɔsɛ ɔkpɛ ɔɖe.”
translated: And Jesus said to them, “Follow me and I will make you fishers of men.” Immediately they left their nets and followed him.

text: Ɔraɖui ɔwɛ̃ ɔɣo i kuposɛ̃ ndɛ̃ ne ɔso gɔ ɔre ne, kuɣa kufu wũ sɔ ɔ̃iwo ɔmɔ fiɛ ɔ̃aɣɔ̃ abi.
translated: The old man went to the farm with his son and his wife, and when they got there, the son said to his father that he wanted to go and defecate.

ref: You Pharisees and teachers of the Law of Moses are in for trouble! You're nothing but show-offs. You lock people out of the kingdom of heaven. You won't go in yourselves, and you keep others from going in.
text: Ndzo miɖe mitã mi mmara matedze gu ma Farisise! Miɖɔɛ Ɣaa i anɔmi anɔmi ame kere! Alasɔ misɛ miɖe makpise mare amumu, fiɛ misɛ mikparama kayi dzoroo i iteso ame. Nwagbe ɔso ne, mi kutsue ikpadzɛ̃ to iamɔ!
translated: Oh blind Pharisees! You clean the outside of the cup and dish, but inside they are full of greed and self-indulgence! Blind Pharisee, first clean the inside of the cup and dish, and then the outside also will be clean. You snakes, how can you say good things when you are evil?

translated: And the angel said to them, ‘Do not be afraid. I bring you good news that will cause great joy for all the people. Today in the town of David a Savior has been born to you; he is the Messiah, the Lord.’

text: Gbaã nto loɣere-ɔ sɔ ato aawe i iyo karɛkɔ̃ gbɔgbɔɔgbɔ ɔwi gɔ atã kuso gɔ ato kapɛrɛ tsɔra aro. Ara Ite I Asɔrɔ̃ra Iso.

text: Ɣɛɛ ne, idaawe i mi ame ngbɔ, si mi ndɛ̃ ɔrere to ɔbie sikpakpa ne, ɔbara so sumudze ɔtã matsɔra.
translated: When I went to the farm, I saw a python and a monitor lizard fighting, and the python swallowed the monitor lizard.

text: Alasɔ ɔbiara gɔ loakpɛ̃ i ɔ̃ ngbã iti ne, ɔto ɔ̃ayura me, ɣɛɛ ngɔ loayura ɔ̃ ngbã ala wũ ɔso ne, ɔto ɔ̃ana ngbã.
translated: When all the people went to the farm, I also went, but I didn't meet anyone on the farm, so I came back home.

text: Fiɛ si ɔnya wũ ne, nto loɣere mi sɔ ɔ̃ana isoɣɔ i ɔwɛ̃ gɔ loyu ngbe iso ɔɖo kasiwɛ̃-kaiwɛ̃ ma loiyu.
